title: Needles for measurement of body fluid analytes such as glucose
abstract: Microneedles for analysis of analytes in body fluids may be manufactured from robust materials such as stainless steel by laser cutting at least the basic needle configuration, applying a coating to electrically insulate and/or assure biocompatibility of the needle, and forming electrodes on the needles via lithography or other processes (preferably after treatment of the coaled areas at which the electrodes are to be applied). Preferably, the needles have a depression formed therein from which the electrodes extend, and which contains a sensing medium which produces a measurable output in response to the local concentration of the analyte of interest Thus, when a needle is inserted into the skin such that the depression and its sensing medium encounters body fluids, analyte measurements can be obtained via the electrodes.
claims: Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. A needle including: a. a needle length L extending from a needle tail to a needle tip, b. a needle thickness T along the needle length between a needle front and an opposing needle rear, wherein T is less than 0.5 mm; c. a needle width W along the needle length between opposing needle sides, each needle side being between the needle front and needle rear, wherein W is less than 0.5 mm, d. electrodes extending along the needle length, each electrode extending from an electrode terminal adjacent the needle tip toward the needle tail. 2. The needle of claim 1 wherein L is less than 3 mm. 3. The needle of claim 1 further including a sensing medium situated on a portion of the outer surface of the needle atop the electrode terminals, the sensing medium being configured to generate a response when exposed to a selected analyte. 4, The needle of claim 3 wherein the sensing medium is situated within a depression indented within the outer surface of the needle. 5, The needle of claim 1 further including: a. a depression indented within the outer surface of the needle, wherein the electrode terminals are situated within the depression, and b. a sensing medium within the depression, the sensing medium being configured to generate a response when exposed to a selected analyte. 6. The needle of claim 3 or claim 5 wherein the selected analyte is glucose. 7. The needle of claim 3 or claim 5 wherein: a. a major portion of the cross-sectional area of the needle is formed of metal, and b. a major portion of the outer surface of the needle has an electrically nonconductive first surface layer thereon, between the sensing medium and the needle tail. 8. The needle of claim 7 wherein: a. the electrodes extend atop the first layer, and b. beneath an electrically nonconductive second surface layer, between the sensing medium and the needle tail. 8. The needle of any preceding claim: a. further including a mounting tab affixed to the needle tail, the mounting tab having a width greater than the needle length L, b. wherein each electrode: (1) extends from the needle onto the mounting tab, and (2) enlarges in at least one dimension on the mounting tab. 9. A method of making the needle of any preceding claim including the step of: a. applying an electrically nonconductive first surface layer to a needle blank; and b. applying the electrodes to the needle blank atop the first surface layer. 10. The method of making the needle of claim 9 further including the step of providing an electrically nonconductive second surface layer atop the electrodes except at the electrode terminals, thereby forming a depression indented within the second surface layer along the outer surface of the needle and above the electrode terminals. 11, The method of making the needle of claim 10 wherein the step of providing an electrically nonconductive second surface layer atop the electrodes except at the electrode terminals includes the steps of: a. applying the second surface layer atop the electrodes and their electrode terminals, and b. removing the second surface layer atop the electrodes. 12. The method of making the needle of claim 11 wherein the step of removing the second surface layer atop the electrodes includes irradiating the second surface layer through an aperture in a mask. 13, The method of making the needle of claim 10 further including the step of removing the first surface layer, electrodes, and second surface layer from the needle blank. 14. The method of making the needle of claim 9 wherein: a. a depression is formed within the needle blank prior to applying the first surface layer to the needle blank, and b. the electrode terminals are situated within the depression. 15. The method of making the needle of claim 14 wherein the depression is milled within a portion of the needle thickness T of the needle blank. 16. The method of making the needle of claim 14: a. further including the step of forming the needle blank prior to applying the first surface layer to the needle blank, b. wherein: (1) the needle blank forming step includes the step of bonding a first needle blank layer to a second needle blank layer, wherein the depression: (a) extends through the first needle blank layer, and (b) bottoms out at the second needle blank layer. 17. The method of making the needle of any of claims 9-16, wherein a sensing medium is applied within the depression atop the electrode terminals, the sensing medium being configured to generate a response when exposed to a selected analyte. 18. The method of making the needle of any preceding claim wherein the step of applying the electrodes includes the step of situating a mask adjacent the first surface layer, wherein the applied electrodes have borders conforming to an aperture in the mask. 19. The method of making the needle of any preceding claim wherein the needle blank includes multiple needles therein, each needle being made as per the preceding claim.
